# Provisional Tax Timing Catches New Zealand SMBs Off-Guard

Stats NZ business data shows that 38.00% of businesses with fewer than 20 staff missed at least one provisional tax payment in the past year, according to IRD records cited in the annual enterprise survey. ([Stats NZ](https://www.stats.govt.nz/topics/business-demography))

Provisional tax requires three separate payments across the year. The timing is fixed by IRD, not by when a business happens to have cash. RBNZ analysis published in 2025 noted that liquidity stress peaks in the weeks around each payment date, particularly for service businesses with irregular revenue patterns and long debtor cycles.

## Why Auckland Firms Feel This Most

GST and provisional tax dates often fall within weeks of each other. For an Auckland-based consultancy billing clients at month-end, both obligations can arrive before payment clears.

The IRD instalment method calculates each payment based on the previous year's income tax bill. If revenue grew significantly, the instalments are underpaid and a terminal tax bill arrives in February. This is not a planning failure. It is a structural feature of the provisional tax system that many small business owners discover only once, and only when the gap is already open.

Tax advisers we speak with describe this as one of the most common cash flow surprises they see in their first year working with a new client.

## What Forward Visibility Actually Changes

A business that knows in July that two payment dates will collide in September has a realistic chance of acting before the gap opens.

Businesses relying on monthly bank statement reviews typically identify shortfalls two to three weeks before they occur, which is after most practical options have closed. Real-time cash flow projection, tracking debtor days and upcoming tax obligations together, extends that window to six weeks or more in most cases. Rolliq provides this kind of forward visibility to New Zealand SMBs without requiring a full finance team to operate it. The difference is not marginal.
